item.title: The Child Who Shocked Investigators
item.description: In 2007, Paris Bennett was 13 years old when he was convicted in connection with the death of his younger sister in Texas. The case drew international attention because of Bennett’s age and the psychological evaluations that followed. It continues to be discussed in relation to juvenile justice, family dynamics, and mental health.

media:content.media:title: The Child Who Shocked Investigators
media:content.media:description: In 2007, Paris Bennett was 13 years old when he was convicted in connection with the death of his younger sister in Texas. The case drew international attention because of Bennett’s age and the psychological evaluations that followed. It continues to be discussed in relation to juvenile justice, family dynamics, and mental health.

item.title: Inside One Of Florida’s Most Unusual Criminal Cases
item.description: In 2016, Austin Harrouff became the focus of a nationally followed investigation after a fatal attack in Florida. The case drew widespread attention because of Harrouff’s unusual behavior, mental health claims, and conflicting expert testimony. He later entered a plea of not guilty by reason of insanity and was committed to a secure state hospital.

media:content.media:title: Inside One Of Florida’s Most Unusual Criminal Cases
media:content.media:description: In 2016, Austin Harrouff became the focus of a nationally followed investigation after a fatal attack in Florida. The case drew widespread attention because of Harrouff’s unusual behavior, mental health claims, and conflicting expert testimony. He later entered a plea of not guilty by reason of insanity and was committed to a secure state hospital.

item.title: The Secret Life That Destroyed Everything
item.description: In 2007, Stephen Grant reported his wife, Tara Lynn Grant, missing from their Michigan home. As investigators examined inconsistencies in his account, the case quickly shifted from a missing person investigation to a high profile criminal prosecution. Grant later pleaded guilty and was sentenced to prison.

media:content.media:title: The Secret Life That Destroyed Everything
media:content.media:description: In 2007, Stephen Grant reported his wife, Tara Lynn Grant, missing from their Michigan home. As investigators examined inconsistencies in his account, the case quickly shifted from a missing person investigation to a high profile criminal prosecution. Grant later pleaded guilty and was sentenced to prison.

item.title: How Phil Spector’s Legacy Changed Forever
item.description: Phil Spector revolutionized popular music with his influential "Wall of Sound" production style before becoming the focus of a high profile criminal case. In 2009, he was convicted in connection with the death of actress Lana Clarkson and sentenced to prison, permanently altering his public legacy.

media:content.media:title: How Phil Spector’s Legacy Changed Forever
media:content.media:description: Phil Spector revolutionized popular music with his influential "Wall of Sound" production style before becoming the focus of a high profile criminal case. In 2009, he was convicted in connection with the death of actress Lana Clarkson and sentenced to prison, permanently altering his public legacy.

item.title: The Disturbing Case Of Shelly Knotek
item.description: Shelly Knotek maintained an outwardly ordinary life while prosecutors said she subjected family members and others in her home to years of abuse and control. The investigation ultimately linked her to multiple deaths, leading to a plea agreement and a lengthy prison sentence. The case continues to be examined for its warning signs and lasting impact on survivors.

media:content.media:title: The Disturbing Case Of Shelly Knotek
media:content.media:description: Shelly Knotek maintained an outwardly ordinary life while prosecutors said she subjected family members and others in her home to years of abuse and control. The investigation ultimately linked her to multiple deaths, leading to a plea agreement and a lengthy prison sentence. The case continues to be examined for its warning signs and lasting impact on survivors.

item.title: The Housekeeper Who Hid A Deadly Secret
item.description: In 1879, Kate Webster was convicted in one of Victorian England’s most infamous criminal cases after the death of her employer, Julia Martha Thomas. The investigation uncovered deception, stolen property, and an attempt to conceal the crime. Webster was later executed, and the case remains a notable part of British criminal history.

media:content.media:title: The Housekeeper Who Hid A Deadly Secret
media:content.media:description: In 1879, Kate Webster was convicted in one of Victorian England’s most infamous criminal cases after the death of her employer, Julia Martha Thomas. The investigation uncovered deception, stolen property, and an attempt to conceal the crime. Webster was later executed, and the case remains a notable part of British criminal history.

item.title: The London Case That Revealed A Hidden Double Life
item.description: Anthony Hardy became the focus of a major London investigation after the remains of several women were discovered in 2002. Detectives uncovered a pattern of targeting vulnerable victims, leading to his conviction in 2003. The case remains one of the United Kingdom’s most widely known criminal investigations involving a repeat offender.

media:content.media:title: The London Case That Revealed A Hidden Double Life
media:content.media:description: Anthony Hardy became the focus of a major London investigation after the remains of several women were discovered in 2002. Detectives uncovered a pattern of targeting vulnerable victims, leading to his conviction in 2003. The case remains one of the United Kingdom’s most widely known criminal investigations involving a repeat offender.
